decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly deploy powerful creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Skyclave Apparition and The Wandering Emperor, backed up by efficient mana dorks like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic. Collected Company provides a way to quickly refill the board with threats, while cards like Ouroboroid and Enduring Innocence offer resilience and protection. Overall, the deck looks to seize control of the game early and maintain pressure to secure a victory.

The deck revolves around using Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a powerful card advantage engine, supported by a diverse range of multicolored spells to maximize its ability. The main focus is controlling the board using removal and counterspells, while also ramping up to play powerful threats like Omnath, Locus of Creation. The deck also includes a Yorion, Sky Nomad companion for additional value. By utilizing the versatility of Bring to Light and Leyline Binding, the deck aims to outvalue and outpace the opponent in a long game, while maintaining a strong control over the battlefield.

The deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, supported by mana acceleration from cards like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves. Collected Company helps to refill the board with additional threats. Skyclave Apparition and Enduring Innocence provide removal options to clear the way for attacks. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive elements, aiming to win quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using low-cost spells and creatures. By efficiently utilizing cards like Fiery Impulse and Cori-Steel Cutter, along with creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, the deck aims to control the board and maintain pressure on the opponent. Additionally, the deck includes card draw and utility spells to ensure consistency and flexibility in gameplay. The inclusion of red and blue mana sources allows for a versatile range of spells and creatures to be played, enabling the deck to adapt to various situations.

The deck focuses on quickly building a strong board presence with cheap creatures and mana dorks, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. Skyclave Apparition offers removal options, while Collected Company provides card advantage and additional threats. The deck also includes some protection in the form of Enduring Innocence and Elspeth, Storm Slayer. Overall, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and disruptive elements to secure a fast victory.

The primary focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruption spells. By utilizing cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to slow down the opponent's game plan, combined with efficient creatures like Skyclave Apparition and Llanowar Elves to apply pressure, this deck aims to create a fast and powerful board presence. Collected Company provides additional creature advantage, while cards like Abandoned Air Temple and Boseiju, Who Endures offer protection and additional utility. Overall, this deck excels at establishing early dominance and maintaining pressure throughout the game.

The deck focuses on using powerful control elements to disrupt and dismantle the opponent's strategy while steadily building up a board presence with resilient threats. By combining disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Duress with removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, the deck aims to control the game until it can establish a dominant position with cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Professor Dellian Fel. The deck also utilizes lands like Mutavault and Boseiju, Who Endures to provide additional flexibility and resilience in various situations.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creature spells like Cori-Steel Cutter and Gran-Gran, while also disrupting opponents with spells like Divide by Zero and Spell Pierce. The deck utilizes card draw and filtering cards like Consider and Accumulate Wisdom to maintain momentum and find key cards. By utilizing a mix of aggression and control, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of creature damage and spell disruption, ultimately aiming to close out the game quickly.

The deck focuses on using a combination of removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push, along with disruptive cards like Thoughtseize to control the game. It also utilizes powerful creatures like Emeritus of Abundance and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to maintain board presence and pressure the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Culling Ritual and Boseiju, Who Endures provide additional utility and resilience against different strategies. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a strong board presence to secure victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through removal spells and hand disruption while using powerful creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to maintain pressure on the opponent. The deck also utilizes lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth to enhance its control elements and provide additional utility. By disrupting the opponent's game plan and establishing a strong board presence, this deck aims to outlast its opponents and emerge victorious in the long game.

This deck focuses on utilizing Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a powerful control commander, casting a wide variety of multicolored spells to gain advantages and control the game. Leyline Binding helps to lock down opponents' key threats, while Bring to Light allows for flexible spell tutoring. The deck includes a mix of removal spells, value creatures, and ramp spells to support Niv-Mizzet's ability to draw cards and deal damage. The addition of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion provides additional value and synergies with the deck's blink effects.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push, as well as hand disruption with Duress and Thoughtseize. The deck also utilizes powerful creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to maintain board presence and pressure the opponent. The lands are carefully chosen to support the strategy, providing a mix of mana fixing and utility. The deck aims to overpower the opponent through resource management and strategic plays, ultimately leading to victory through attrition.

This deck focuses on using the synergy between Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards like Esika's Chariot and Overlord of the Balemurk, the deck can create a wide range of threats that are difficult for the opponent to deal with. The inclusion of Thoughtseize and Collective Brutality helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan, allowing the combo pieces to shine. The deck also includes some utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Takenuma, Abandoned Mire to provide additional value in certain matchups.

This deck focuses on using control elements like discard spells, removal, and powerful creatures to dominate the game. It utilizes cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's game plan while stabilizing with threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistency, with a mix of dual lands to ensure smooth casting of spells. Overall, the strategy revolves around controlling the board state and outvaluing the opponent through a combination of disruption and powerful creatures.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures. The key strategy is to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, while gradually building up your own board presence with creatures like Professor Dellian Fel and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck aims to grind out value over time and outlast the opponent's threats, ultimately coming out on top with a well-timed finisher like Maelstrom Pulse or Sheoldred's Edict.

This Pioneer 1 vs 1 deck focuses on aggressive strategies with a mix of red and blue spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter, along with burn spells like Fiery Impulse, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and control the battlefield. With card draw options like Consider and Sleight of Hand, along with tempo plays like Boomerang Basics and Riverpyre Verge, the deck can maintain momentum and keep the pressure on the opponent.
